name: google-calendar-webhook
description: Receive real-time push notifications from Google Calendar when events are created, updated, or deleted.

CALENDAR_ID.description: Google Calendar ID to watch for changes (e.g. 'primary' or 'user@example.com')

OAUTH_TOKEN.description: Secret key_path for a Google OAuth2 refresh token with calendar.readonly scope (e.g. 'google/calendar_oauth_token')

EVENT_TYPES.description: Calendar change types to forward downstream. Omit to receive all types.

setup_instructions.title: Enable the Google Calendar API
setup_instructions.detail: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com, create or select a project, and enable the Google Calendar API under 'APIs & Services > Library'.

setup_instructions.title: Create OAuth credentials
setup_instructions.detail: In 'APIs & Services > Credentials', create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Web application). Add your Talos redirect URI. Authorize with the 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly' scope.

setup_instructions.title: Store the refresh token as a secret
setup_instructions.detail: Use set_secret with key_path='google/calendar_oauth_token' and the resulting OAuth2 refresh token as the value.

setup_instructions.title: Configure this node
setup_instructions.detail: Set CALENDAR_ID to 'primary' or your specific calendar address, and OAUTH_TOKEN to 'google/calendar_oauth_token'. Optionally restrict EVENT_TYPES to only the change kinds you care about.
